memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_line_count

detaileddescription.para:
help For more information on buffers, see |buffers|.
Unloaded Buffers: ~
Buffers may be unloaded by the |:bunload| command or the buffer's |'bufhidden'| option. When a buffer is unloaded its file contents are freed from memory and vim cannot operate on the buffer lines until it is reloaded (usually by opening the buffer again in a new window). API methods such as |nvim_buf_get_lines()| and |nvim_buf_line_count()| will be affected.
You can use |nvim_buf_is_loaded()| or |nvim_buf_line_count()| to check whether a buffer is loaded. Returns the number of lines in the given buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

simplesect.para: Line count, or 0 for unloaded buffer. |api-buffer|

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Buffer buffer, Boolean send_buffer, Dict(buf_attach) *opts, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(4)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_attach

detaileddescription.para: Activates buffer-update events on a channel, or as Lua callbacks.
Example (Lua): capture buffer updates in a global events variable (use "vim.print(events)" to see its contents):

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

True if the initial notification should contain the whole buffer: first notification will be nvim_buf_lines_event. Else the first notification will be nvim_buf_changedtick_event. Not for Lua callbacks.

Optional parameters.
on_lines: Lua callback invoked on change. Return a truthy value (not false or nil) to detach. Args:
the string "lines"
buffer id
b:changedtick
first line that changed (zero-indexed)
last line that was changed
last line in the updated range
byte count of previous contents
deleted_codepoints (if utf_sizes is true)
deleted_codeunits (if utf_sizes is true)
on_bytes: Lua callback invoked on change. This callback receives more granular information about the change compared to on_lines. Return a truthy value (not false or nil) to detach. Args:
the string "bytes"
buffer id
b:changedtick
start row of the changed text (zero-indexed)
start column of the changed text
byte offset of the changed text (from the start of the buffer)
old end row of the changed text (offset from start row)
old end column of the changed text (if old end row = 0, offset from start column)
old end byte length of the changed text
new end row of the changed text (offset from start row)
new end column of the changed text (if new end row = 0, offset from start column)
new end byte length of the changed text
on_changedtick: Lua callback invoked on changedtick increment without text change. Args:
the string "changedtick"
buffer id
b:changedtick
on_detach: Lua callback invoked on detach. Args:
the string "detach"
buffer id
on_reload: Lua callback invoked on reload. The entire buffer content should be considered changed. Args:
the string "reload"
buffer id
utf_sizes: include UTF-32 and UTF-16 size of the replaced region, as args to on_lines.
preview: also attach to command preview (i.e. 'inccommand') events.

simplesect.para: False if attach failed (invalid parameter, or buffer isn't loaded); otherwise True. TODO: LUA_API_NO_EVAL

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Buffer buffer, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(4) FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_detach

detaileddescription.para: Deactivates buffer-update events on the channel.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

simplesect.para: False if detach failed (because the buffer isn't loaded); otherwise True.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Buffer buffer, Integer start, Integer end, Boolean strict_indexing, Arena *arena, lua_State *lstate, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_get_lines

detaileddescription.para: Gets a line-range from the buffer.
Indexing is zero-based, end-exclusive. Negative indices are interpreted as length+1+index: -1 refers to the index past the end. So to get the last element use start=-2 and end=-1.
Out-of-bounds indices are clamped to the nearest valid value, unless strict_indexing is set.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer
Whether out-of-bounds should be an error.

simplesect.para: Array of lines, or empty array for unloaded buffer.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Buffer buffer, Integer start, Integer end, Boolean strict_indexing, ArrayOf(String) replacement, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_TEXTLOCK_ALLOW_CMDWIN
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_set_lines

detaileddescription.para: Sets (replaces) a line-range in the buffer.
Indexing is zero-based, end-exclusive. Negative indices are interpreted as length+1+index: -1 refers to the index past the end. So to change or delete the last line use start=-2 and end=-1.
To insert lines at a given index, set start and end to the same index. To delete a range of lines, set replacement to an empty array.
Out-of-bounds indices are clamped to the nearest valid value, unless strict_indexing is set.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer
Whether out-of-bounds should be an error.
Array of lines to use as replacement

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Buffer buffer, Integer start_row, Integer start_col, Integer end_row, Integer end_col, ArrayOf(String) replacement, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_TEXTLOCK_ALLOW_CMDWIN
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_set_text

detaileddescription.para: Sets (replaces) a range in the buffer
This is recommended over |nvim_buf_set_lines()| when only modifying parts of a line, as extmarks will be preserved on non-modified parts of the touched lines.
Indexing is zero-based. Row indices are end-inclusive, and column indices are end-exclusive.
To insert text at a given (row, column) location, use start_row = end_row = row and start_col = end_col = col. To delete the text in a range, use replacement = {}.

simplesect.para: Prefer |nvim_buf_set_lines()| (for performance) to add or delete entire lines.
Prefer |nvim_paste()| or |nvim_put()| to insert (instead of replace) text at cursor.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer
Starting column (byte offset) on first line
Ending column (byte offset) on last line, exclusive
Array of lines to use as replacement

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Buffer buffer, Integer start_row, Integer start_col, Integer end_row, Integer end_col, Dict(empty) *opts, Arena *arena, lua_State *lstate, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(9)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_get_text

detaileddescription.para: Gets a range from the buffer (may be partial lines, unlike |nvim_buf_get_lines()|).
Indexing is zero-based. Row indices are end-inclusive, and column indices are end-exclusive.
Prefer |nvim_buf_get_lines()| when retrieving entire lines.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer
Starting column (byte offset) on first line
Ending column (byte offset) on last line, exclusive

simplesect.para: Array of lines, or empty array for unloaded buffer.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Integer index, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(5)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_get_offset

detaileddescription.para: Returns the byte offset of a line (0-indexed). |api-indexing|

Line 1 (index=0) has offset 0. UTF-8 bytes are counted. EOL is one byte. 'fileformat' and 'fileencoding' are ignored. The line index just after the last line gives the total byte-count of the buffer. A final EOL byte is counted if it would be written, see 'eol'.
Unlike |line2byte()|, throws error for out-of-bounds indexing. Returns -1 for unloaded buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

simplesect.para: Integer byte offset, or -1 for unloaded buffer.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, String name, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_get_var

detaileddescription.para: Gets a buffer-scoped (b:) variable.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(2)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_get_changedtick

detaileddescription.para: Gets a changed tick of a buffer

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, String mode, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(3)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_get_keymap

detaileddescription.para: Gets a list of buffer-local |mapping| definitions.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

simplesect.para: Array of |maparg()|-like dictionaries describing mappings. The "buffer" key holds the associated buffer id.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Buffer buffer, String mode, String lhs, String rhs, Dict(keymap) *opts, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(6)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_set_keymap

detaileddescription.para: Sets a buffer-local |mapping| for the given mode.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Buffer buffer, String mode, String lhs, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(6)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_del_keymap

detaileddescription.para: Unmaps a buffer-local |mapping| for the given mode.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, String name, Object value, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_set_var

detaileddescription.para: Sets a buffer-scoped (b:) variable

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, String name, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_del_var

detaileddescription.para: Removes a buffer-scoped (b:) variable

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_get_name

detaileddescription.para: Gets the full file name for the buffer

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, String name, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_set_name

detaileddescription.para: Sets the full file name for a buffer, like |:file_f|

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer) FUNC_API_SINCE(5)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_is_loaded

detaileddescription.para: Checks if a buffer is valid and loaded. See |api-buffer| for more info about unloaded buffers.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

simplesect.para: true if the buffer is valid and loaded, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Dict(buf_delete) *opts, Error *err) FUNC_API_TEXTLOCK
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_delete

detaileddescription.para: Deletes the buffer. See |:bwipeout|

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

Optional parameters. Keys:
force: Force deletion and ignore unsaved changes.
unload: Unloaded only, do not delete. See |:bunload|

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer) FUNC_API_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_is_valid

detaileddescription.para: Checks if a buffer is valid.

simplesect.para: Even if a buffer is valid it may have been unloaded. See |api-buffer| for more info about unloaded buffers.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

simplesect.para: true if the buffer is valid, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, String name, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(8)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_del_mark

detaileddescription.para: Deletes a named mark in the buffer. See |mark-motions|.

simplesect.para: only deletes marks set in the buffer, if the mark is not set in the buffer it will return false.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer to set the mark on

simplesect.para: true if the mark was deleted, else false.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, String name, Integer line, Integer col, Dict(empty) *opts, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(8)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_set_mark

detaileddescription.para: Sets a named mark in the given buffer, all marks are allowed file/uppercase, visual, last change, etc. See |mark-motions|.
Marks are (1,0)-indexed. |api-indexing|

simplesect.para: Passing 0 as line deletes the mark

parameterdescription.para: Buffer to set the mark on
Optional parameters. Reserved for future use.

simplesect.para: true if the mark was set, else false.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, String name, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_get_mark

detaileddescription.para: Returns a (row,col) tuple representing the position of the named mark. "End of line" column position is returned as |v:maxcol| (big number). See |mark-motions|.
Marks are (1,0)-indexed. |api-indexing|

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

simplesect.para: (row, col) tuple, (0, 0) if the mark is not set, or is an uppercase/file mark set in another buffer.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, LuaRef fun, Error *err) FUNC_API_LUA_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_call

detaileddescription.para: Call a function with buffer as temporary current buffer.

This temporarily switches current buffer to "buffer". If the current window already shows "buffer", the window is not switched. If a window inside the current tabpage (including a float) already shows the buffer, then one of those windows will be set as current window temporarily. Otherwise a temporary scratch window (called the "autocmd window" for historical reasons) will be used.
This is useful e.g. to call Vimscript functions that only work with the current buffer/window currently, like jobstart(., {'term': v:true}).

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer
Function to call inside the buffer (currently Lua callable only)

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Arena *arena, Error *err)
memberdef.name: nvim__buf_stats

memberdef.argsstring: (win_T *win, linenr_T lo, linenr_T hi, linenr_T extra)
memberdef.name: fix_cursor

memberdef.argsstring: (win_T *win, linenr_T start_row, colnr_T start_col, linenr_T end_row, colnr_T end_col, linenr_T new_rows, colnr_T new_cols_at_end_row)
memberdef.name: fix_cursor_cols

detaileddescription.para: Fix cursor position after replacing text between (start_row, start_col) and (end_row, end_col).
win->w_cursor.lnum is assumed to be >= start_row and <= end_row.

memberdef.argsstring: (lua_State *lstate, Array *a, size_t size, Arena *arena)
memberdef.name: init_line_array

detaileddescription.para:
Initialise a string array either:
on the Lua stack (as a table) (if lstate is not NULL)
as an API array object (if lstate is NULL).

parameterdescription.para: Lua state. When NULL the Array is initialized instead.

memberdef.argsstring: (lua_State *lstate, Array *a, const char *s, size_t len, int idx, bool replace_nl, Arena *arena)
memberdef.name: push_linestr

detaileddescription.para: Push a string onto either the Lua stack (as a table element) or an API array object.
For Lua, a table of the correct size must be created first. API array objects must be pre allocated.

parameterdescription.para: Lua state. When NULL the Array is pushed to instead.
Array to push onto when not using Lua
0-based index to place s (only used for Lua)

memberdef.argsstring: (buf_T *buf, size_t n, linenr_T start, int start_idx, bool replace_nl, Array *l, lua_State *lstate, Arena *arena)
memberdef.name: buf_collect_lines

detaileddescription.para: Collects n buffer lines into array l and/or lua_State lstate, optionally replacing newlines with NUL.

parameterdescription.para: First index to push to (only used for Lua)
If not NULL, Lines are copied here
If not NULL, Lines are pushed into a table onto the stack
